tests.scenario: Free tier user completes a mood entry without hitting a paywall
steps: Create a new free account
Log in to the system with the free account credentials
Navigate to the mood tracking section
Complete and submit a full mood entry
tests.expected_result: The mood entry is saved successfully and no paywall prompt appears during the process.
tests.notes: The original just said 'access basic features' which is way too vague — changed it to actually check there's no paywall.

tests.scenario: Free tier user views mood history for the past 30 days without paywall
steps: Log in with a free account that has at least one mood entry
Navigate to the mood history section
Observe whether a paywall appears
tests.expected_result: The user can view their mood history for the past 30 days and no subscription prompt appears.
tests.notes: The auto version tested premium restriction which isn't what this requirement is about. Swapped it out for a 30-day history check.

tests.scenario: Pricing summary is shown before account creation
steps: Open the app for the first time
Navigate toward the account creation screen
Observe whether a pricing summary is displayed before any account fields appear
tests.expected_result: A pricing summary listing at least three free features and the monthly cost of the premium subscription is visible before the user submits any account information.
tests.notes: Auto version checked pricing inside account settings which is too late. Users need to see this before they even sign up.

tests.scenario: No pricing information shown before account creation
steps: Open the app for the first time
Proceed directly to the account creation screen
Check whether any pricing or feature breakdown is shown before submitting account details
tests.expected_result: If no pricing summary is displayed before account submission the test fails. The system must show pricing before account creation.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: Mood check-in screen displays at least ten labeled emotion options
steps: Log in to the system
Navigate to the mood check-in screen
Count the number of labeled emotion options displayed
tests.expected_result: At least ten labeled emotion options are visible or accessible by scrolling, including both positive and negative emotional states.
tests.notes: The auto test was about logging a mood and viewing history — totally different thing. Changed it to actually count the emotion options.

tests.scenario: Emotion options include both positive and negative states
steps: Open the mood check-in screen
Review all available emotion labels
Categorize each as positive or negative
tests.expected_result: The emotion list contains at least one clearly positive emotion and at least one clearly negative emotion among the ten or more options.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: Check-in question differs on consecutive days
steps: Open the app and note the check-in question displayed on day 1
Complete the check-in
Return to the check-in screen on day 2
Note the check-in question displayed
tests.expected_result: The check-in question on day 2 is different from the question shown on day 1.
tests.notes: Auto test was about a reflection prompt library which had nothing to do with this group. Replaced it with an actual consecutive day question check.

tests.scenario: Check-in question varies across seven consecutive days
steps: Record the check-in question shown each day for seven consecutive days
Compare each day's question against the previous day's question
tests.expected_result: No two consecutive days share the same check-in question across all seven days.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: Free tier user views 30-day mood history graph
steps: Log in with a free account that has at least seven mood entries
Navigate to the mood history screen
Observe the graph displayed
tests.expected_result: A graphical mood history covering up to 30 days is displayed with no subscription prompt appearing.
tests.notes: Pretty much the same test, just added a check that no paywall shows up for free users.

tests.scenario: User views mood history with fewer than seven entries
steps: Log in with a free account that has fewer than seven mood entries
Navigate to the mood history screen
tests.expected_result: The system displays whatever entries exist or shows an empty state message. No paywall prompt appears.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: User receives insight summary after seven consecutive check-ins
steps: Log in to the application
Complete mood check-ins for seven consecutive days
Navigate to the insights section
tests.expected_result: The system displays a summary containing at least three observations about the user's mood patterns.
tests.notes: Changed '3-5 recommendations' to 'at least three observations' — recommendations felt too prescriptive and hard to verify.

tests.scenario: User does not receive insights before completing seven check-ins
steps: Log in to the application
Complete mood check-ins for fewer than seven days
Navigate to the insights section
tests.expected_result: The system does not display a personalized insight summary and shows a message indicating more check-ins are needed.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: User selects a non-English language during onboarding
steps: Open the app for the first time
Proceed through the onboarding screens
Locate the language selection screen
Select a non-English language
Continue through the remaining onboarding steps
tests.expected_result: All UI elements, prompts, and content displayed after language selection are shown in the selected language.
tests.notes: Brand new test — the automated pipeline skipped language support entirely even though it was its own review group.

tests.scenario: Onboarding does not offer a language selection screen
steps: Open the app for the first time
Proceed through all onboarding screens
Check whether a language selection screen appeared
tests.expected_result: A language selection screen must appear during onboarding offering at least one non-English language. If absent the test fails.
tests.notes: Brand new negative case — same reason as above.

tests.scenario: Language preference persists after app is closed and reopened
steps: Complete onboarding and select a non-English language
Close the application completely
Reopen the application
tests.expected_result: The app launches in the previously selected language without prompting the user to re-select.
tests.notes: Brand new test — language persistence wasn't covered anywhere in the auto pipeline.

tests.scenario: App reverts to English after language was previously set
steps: Select a non-English language during onboarding
Close and reopen the app
Observe the language displayed on launch
tests.expected_result: If the app launches in English instead of the previously selected language the test fails.
tests.notes: Brand new negative case for language persistence.

tests.scenario: User views third-party data disclosure in privacy settings
steps: Log in to the app
Navigate to the privacy settings section
Open the data disclosure page
tests.expected_result: A list of all third-party data recipients is displayed, each with the category of data shared and a link to their privacy policy.
tests.notes: Auto version was about data restoration after updates which has nothing to do with privacy. Completely replaced it.

tests.scenario: Privacy disclosure page is missing or incomplete
steps: Navigate to the privacy settings section
Open the data disclosure page
Check whether third-party recipients are listed with data categories and policy links
tests.expected_result: If any third-party recipient is listed without a data category or policy link the test fails.
tests.notes: did not change

tests.scenario: App prompts for PIN before displaying personal data
steps: Enable PIN lock in the app settings
Close the app completely
Reopen the app
tests.expected_result: The system prompts the user to enter their PIN before displaying any journal or mood data.
tests.notes: Auto version tested repetitive questions which belongs to a completely different group. Swapped it for a PIN lock test.

tests.scenario: Personal data is visible without authentication when PIN is enabled
steps: Enable PIN lock in the app settings
Resume the app from the background without authenticating
Check whether any journal or mood data is visible
tests.expected_result: If any personal data is visible before PIN authentication succeeds the test fails.
tests.notes: did not change
